name: X-ray Diffraction Analysis
description: X-ray diffraction probes crystal structure: X-rays scatter off lattice planes and interfere constructively only when Bragg's law is satisfied, producing peaks versus 2-theta that are indexed and refined (e.g. Rietveld) to determine the unit cell and phase.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Crystalline sample"]
    N2["Irradiate with X-rays"]
    N3["Scatter off lattice planes"]
    N4{"Bragg: nλ = 2d sinθ?"}
    N5["Out of phase: no peak"]
    N6["Diffraction peak"]
    N7["Record pattern vs 2θ"]
    N8["Index reflections"]
    N9["Refine structure (Rietveld)"]
    N10["Crystal structure / phase"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N5 --> N7
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The reflection of X-rays by crystals (Bragg's law)
sources.authors: Bragg, W. H.; Bragg, W. L.
sources.journal: Proceedings of the Royal Society A

sources.title: A profile refinement method for nuclear and magnetic structures (Rietveld)
Elements of X-Ray Diffraction, 3rd ed.
sources.authors: Cullity, B. D.; Stock, S. R.
